words.def: held or fixed in position securely; difficult to move, open or separate
words.example: He kept a tight grip on her arm.

words.def: until
words.example: We're open till 6 o'clock.

words.def: a metal container in which food is sold; the contents of one of these containers
words.example: Open the tin and drain the tuna.

words.def: very small in size or amount
words.example: a tiny baby

words.def: a small piece of advice about something practical
words.example: tip on/for doing something, handy tips for buying a computer

words.def: one of the five small parts that stick out from the foot; any similar part on the foot of an animal or bird
words.example: the big/little toe (= the largest/smallest toe)

words.def: the soft part in the mouth that moves around, used for tasting, swallowing, speaking, etc.
words.example: He clicked his tongue to attract their attention.

words.def: completely
words.example: They come from totally different cultures.

words.def: the sense that enables you to be aware of things and what they are like when you put your hands and fingers on them
words.example: the sense of touch

words.def: a journey made for pleasure during which several different towns, countries, etc. are visited
words.example: a walking/sightseeing/bus tour

words.def: the business activity connected with providing accommodation, services and entertainment for people who are visiting a place for pleasure
words.example: The area is heavily dependent on tourism.

words.def: the activity of buying and selling or of exchanging goods or services between people or countries
words.example: international/foreign/global/world trade

words.def: to express the meaning of speech or writing in a different language
words.example: translate something into something, He translated the letter into English.

words.def: a system for carrying people or goods from one place to another using vehicles, roads, etc.
words.example: air/freight/road transport

words.def: to behave in a particular way towards somebody/something
words.example: treat somebody/something with something, to treat people with respect

words.def: something that is done to cure an illness or injury, or to make somebody look and feel good
words.example: The drug is used in the treatment of depression.

words.def: a general direction in which a situation is changing or developing
words.example: social/economic/political/demographic trends

words.def: something that you do to make somebody believe something that is not true, or to annoy somebody as a joke
words.example: They had to think of a trick to get past the guards.

words.def: the true facts about something, rather than the things that have been invented or guessed
words.example: Do you think she's telling the truth?

words.def: a long, hollow pipe made of metal, plastic, rubber, etc., through which liquids or gases move from one place to another
words.example: He had to be fed through a feeding tube for several months.

words.def: used to describe one of a pair of children who are twins
words.example: a twin brother/sister

words.def: a class or group of people or things that share particular qualities or features and are part of a larger group; a kind or sort
words.example: a rare blood type

words.def: having the usual qualities or features of a particular type of person, thing or group
words.example: a typical Italian cafe

words.def: used to say that something usually happens in the way that you are stating
words.example: The standard chips are typically used for databases and other business software.

words.def: a thick rubber ring that fits around the edge of a wheel of a car, bicycle, etc.
words.example: a front/rear tyre

words.def: unpleasant to look at
words.example: an ugly face

words.def: not having the skill, strength, time, knowledge, etc. to do something
words.example: a former soldier who has been unable to find work since the war ended

words.def: not letting you feel physically comfortable; unpleasant to wear, sit on, etc.
words.example: uncomfortable shoes

words.def: without a job although able to work
words.example: How long have you been unemployed?

words.def: the fact of a number of people not having a job; the number of people without a job
words.example: an area of high/low unemployment

words.def: not right or fair according to a set of rules or principles; not treating people equally
words.example: They had been given an unfair advantage.

words.def: an organization of workers, usually in a particular industry, that exists to protect their interests, improve conditions of work, etc.
words.example: I've joined the union.

words.def: used to say that something can only happen or be true in a particular situation
words.example: You won't get paid for time off unless you have a doctor's note.

words.def: different from a particular person or thing
words.example: Music is quite unlike any other art form.

words.def: not likely to happen; not probable
words.example: unlikely to do something, The project seemed unlikely to succeed.

words.def: not needed; more than is needed
words.example: They were found guilty of causing unnecessary suffering to animals.

words.def: not pleasant or comfortable
words.example: an unpleasant experience/surprise/task

words.def: up to the point in time or the event mentioned
words.example: Let's wait until the rain stops.

words.def: unhappy or disappointed because of something unpleasant that has happened
words.example: I understand how upset you must be feeling.

words.def: on a floor of a house or other building that is higher than the one that you are on
words.example: an upstairs room/window/bathroom

words.def: familiar with something because you do it or experience it often
words.example: used to doing something, I'm not used to eating so much at lunchtime.

words.def: worth a lot of money
words.example: My home is my most valuable asset.

words.def: a covered vehicle with no side windows in its back half, usually smaller than a lorry, used for carrying goods or people
words.example: the driver of a white van

words.def: several different sorts of the same thing
words.example: There is a wide variety of patterns to choose from.

words.def: several different
words.example: various types/forms/kinds of somebody/something

words.def: a thing that is used for transporting people or goods from one place to another, such as a car or lorry
words.example: motor vehicles (= cars, buses, lorries, etc.)

words.def: a person who has been attacked, injured or killed as the result of a crime, a disease, an accident, etc.
words.example: shooting/murder victims

words.def: what you can see from a particular place or position, especially beautiful countryside
words.example: view of something, There were magnificent views of the surrounding countryside.

words.def: involving or caused by physical force that is intended to hurt or kill somebody
words.example: violent crime/criminals

words.def: a living thing, too small to be seen without a microscope, that causes disease in people, animals and plants
words.example: the flu/influenza virus

words.def: a formal choice that you make in an election or at a meeting in order to choose somebody or decide something
words.example: to win/lose votes

words.def: at a fairly high temperature in a way that is pleasant, rather than being hot or cold
words.example: warm weather/temperatures/air

words.def: to tell somebody about something, especially something dangerous or unpleasant that is likely to happen, so that they can avoid it
words.example: warn somebody, I tried to warn him, but he wouldn't listen.

words.def: a statement, an event, etc. telling somebody that something bad or unpleasant may happen in the future so that they can try to avoid it
words.example: I had absolutely no warning.
